chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1431 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of main case 24.09.2024 Mr. Sohail Ahmed Khoso, Advocate holds brief for Mr. Alam Sher Khan Bozdar, Advocate for petitioner Mst. Sara Khatoon. Mr. Ubedullah Malano, Advocate for respondents-SEPCO along with Vijay Kumar (PD Construction), Deepak Kumar (Manager Legal) and Ahmed Ali Buriro (DD CA), SEPCO Sukkur. Mr. Nisar Ahmed G. Abro, Deputy Attorney General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- In compliance of orders dated 15.05.2024 and 21.08.2024, through a Statement of learned Counsel for the petitioners, amended title has been filed arraying Mst. Sara Khatoon as petitioner No.2, which is taken on record. A Statement has been field along with two original cheques bearing No.2485255084 and 2485255084 dated 23.09.2024 amounting to Rs.4,18,179/- and Rs.97,750/- respectively in favour of Mst. Sara wd/o late Ahmed Khan (petitioner No.2). The Officer, Deepak Kumar, is present and states that all the inquiries have been favourably concluded to give the petitioners maximum value of post-retirement benefits. He further states that total sum of Rs.15,48,840/- has been divided into two widows as per number of their children. Regarding share of Mst. Basheeran Khatoon (petitioner No.1), he states that Rs.10,32,611/- have been transferred to her Account. The Additional Registrar of this Court is directed to retain the aforesaid two cheques and handover the same to Mst. Sara Khatoon (petitioner No.2). Since purpose of filing this petition has been served, it is disposed of accordingly.
Sara Khatoon (petitioner No.2). Since purpose of filing this petition has been served, it is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
